verdicts.name: display_render_engine
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_08008154 (draw_ui_element, decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:2974+) is a text/UI-element layout+blit engine: allocates per-glyph scratch buffers (memory_alloc), does word-wrap measurement, then blits each glyph via FUN_08008b90 into the in-RAM `_framebuffer_ptr` with viewport clipping. Ours: firmware/src/fonts/font.c:font_draw_string + per-screen UI draw helpers (firmware/src/ui/*). Stock differs: it composites into an off-screen RGB565 framebuffer with its own word-wrap allocator; ours writes glyphs straight to the LCD via lcd_set_window/lcd_write_data, no full framebuffer.
verdicts.justification: Decoded to a framebuffer layout/blit engine; our firmware re-implements UI text layout with its own bitmap-font system and direct-to-LCD writes, so it is justified-not-needed (NA_OUR_OWN), not a gap.

verdicts.name: glyph_render_single
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_08008b90 (decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:3533) is a single-glyph anti-aliased bitmap blitter: unpacks N-bit-per-pixel coverage, alpha-blends fg color against the existing `_framebuffer_ptr` pixel (RGB565 blend math at lines 3588-3591), viewport-clipped. Ours: firmware/src/fonts/font.c:font_draw_char (line 20) renders fixed bitmap glyphs with fg/bg directly to the LCD. Stock differs: it does sub-pixel alpha-blend compositing into a RAM framebuffer; ours is opaque fg/bg straight to LCD memory-mapped data register.
verdicts.justification: Decoded as an AA glyph compositor; ours is an original bitmap-font renderer serving the same role with a different (direct-LCD, non-blended) approach. Justified-not-needed.

verdicts.name: scope_ui_draw_main
verdicts.evidence: Stock draw_oscilloscope_screen @ 08015f50 (decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:4433) is the stock scope screen: a switch on ui_state_flags draws dotted grid lines by poking 0x18c3 directly into `_framebuffer_ptr` with viewport clipping, plus draw_text/draw_ui_element calls. Ours: firmware/src/ui/scope_ui.c:draw_scope_screen (line 847) -> draw_scope_grid (line 76) + trigger/ground/measurement helpers writing via lcd_set_pixel. Stock differs: composites into a RAM framebuffer and uses its layout engine; ours draws directly to the LCD with our own grid/trigger/badge layout.
verdicts.justification: Decoded as the stock scope UI screen; we have an original scope UI (scope_ui.c) with our own layout and direct-LCD draw. NA_OUR_OWN, resolved.

verdicts.name: scope_ui_draw_header_ch2
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_08017514 (decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:5215) is trivial: draw_text(string,0,0) + one draw_ui_element(0x34,0,0xd8,0x14, label[current_mode_index], buf,0xffff,1) — a per-mode header label drawn through the framebuffer layout engine (draw_ui_element @08008154). Ours: header/status rendering in firmware/src/ui/status_bar.c and scope_ui.c using font_draw_string directly to LCD. Stock differs: indexes a stock label table and routes through its framebuffer compositor; ours uses our own strings and direct-LCD font draw.
verdicts.justification: Decoded as a one-line stock header-label draw via the framebuffer UI engine; our status-bar/scope UI covers the same purpose originally. NA_OUR_OWN.

verdicts.name: scope_ui_draw_trigger_info
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_0801819c (decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:5230) sprintf's a value (DAT_20000f14+0x94) into a format buffer then draws three draw_ui_element rows (trigger-related selectable list, highlight color toggled by DAT_20000f14). It is a trigger/config info panel routed through the framebuffer UI engine. Ours: firmware/src/ui/scope_ui.c:draw_trigger_status (line 191) + draw_trigger_indicator (line 103) render trigger level/edge/mode directly to LCD. Stock differs: formats stock label tables into its RAM-framebuffer list widget; ours composes trigger state with our own widgets straight to LCD.
verdicts.justification: Decoded as a stock trigger-info list panel using the framebuffer UI engine; our scope_ui trigger status/indicator serves the purpose with an original implementation. NA_OUR_OWN.

verdicts.name: scope_ui_draw_timebase_list
verdicts.evidence: Stock draw_settings_menu @ 08018324 (decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:5264) draws two selectable menu rows: when not selected it paints a dotted fill (0x18c3) into `_framebuffer_ptr` via nested loops with viewport clipping, then draw_ui_element for each label (string table 0x80bd09b/0x80bc6ae). It is a settings/list menu, framebuffer-composited. Ours: firmware/src/ui/settings_ui.c:draw_menu_item/draw_settings_top/draw_settings_osc render menu rows directly to LCD with our own labels and selection highlight. Stock differs: dotted-fill compositing into RAM framebuffer; ours direct-LCD menu rows.
verdicts.justification: Decoded as the stock settings/list menu (dotted selectable rows) through the framebuffer engine; our settings_ui.c is an original menu implementation. NA_OUR_OWN.

verdicts.name: lcd_draw_pixel
verdicts.evidence: MISLABEL: FUN_08018964 (decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:5391) is NOT an ST7789 pixel write. It is a Wu-style anti-aliased point plot: computes 4 neighbor coverages from fractional x/y and calls FUN_08018cb0 four times, each reading the existing `_framebuffer_ptr` pixel and alpha-blending into the RAM framebuffer (viewport-clipped). It is part of the scope waveform/curve draw (caller FUN_08018af0). Our real LCD pixel write is firmware/src/drivers/lcd.c:lcd_set_pixel (line 459) -> lcd_set_window + lcd_write_data (ST7789 CASET/RASET/RAMWR over EXMC). Stock differs: AA sub-pixel blend into a RAM framebuffer; ours is opaque single-pixel direct to LCD.
verdicts.justification: Name is a mislabel; decoded as an AA framebuffer point-plot for scope curve rendering, not a register primitive. Our scope draw (scope_ui.c) is original and direct-LCD, so justified-not-needed (NA_OUR_OWN), not VERIFY_FAITHFUL.

verdicts.name: lcd_set_window
verdicts.evidence: MISLABEL: FUN_08018af0 (decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:5481) is NOT ST7789 CASET/RASET. It is a parametric curve/arc rasterizer: loops a parameter t from start to end (fmod via FUN_0803c82e), evaluates a coefficient LUT (&DAT_0806e670/0806e674 lerp) to get x/y, and calls the AA point-plot FUN_08018964 for each step — drawing rounded/curved scope decorations into the RAM framebuffer. Our real lcd_set_window is firmware/src/drivers/lcd.c:430 issuing ST7789_CASET(0x2A)/RASET(0x2B)/RAMWR(0x2C) via lcd_write_cmd/lcd_write_data8 over the EXMC bus. Stock differs entirely: this is a curve rasterizer, not a window-address setter.
verdicts.justification: Name is a mislabel; decoded as a parametric-curve rasterizer into the framebuffer, unrelated to ST7789 windowing. Our scope/curve drawing is original; classifying VERIFY_FAITHFUL against our true lcd_set_window would be dishonest. NA_OUR_OWN, resolved.

verdicts.name: lcd_fill_color
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_08018cb0 (decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:5577) writes a single RGB565 pixel into a RAM SOFTWARE framebuffer (_framebuffer_ptr, allocated via memory_alloc at v2.c:3729/3776) with _viewport_* clipping, alpha-blending param_1 against a source color (param_4/param_5) — it is a backbuffer pixel-blend, NOT an LCD/EXMC op. The name 'lcd_fill_color' is a mislabel. Our firmware has no software framebuffer; firmware/src/drivers/lcd.c (lcd_fill_rect, lcd_write_data) writes pixels directly to the EXMC-mapped data register *LCD_DATA_ADDR (0x60020000), and color blending/fills come from our own draw_scope_screen path in main.c.
verdicts.justification: Decoded to a framebuffer-blend primitive; stock uses a RAM backbuffer + alpha blend, we render direct-to-EXMC with our own draw code — intentionally not byte-faithful, justified-not-needed, not a gap.

verdicts.name: scope_draw_grid_vertical
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_08018ea8 (v2.c:5704) is a generic Bresenham LINE rasterizer into the RAM _framebuffer_ptr backbuffer with _viewport clipping (plots start pixel, walks param_2->param_4 in y, plots end pixel) — a general line draw, not specifically a 'vertical grid'. Our firmware draws scope grid/lines directly to EXMC inside firmware/src/main.c draw_scope_screen() and lcd.c primitives, with no software framebuffer and no shared viewport-clip line lib.
verdicts.justification: Decoded as a general framebuffer line routine; our grid drawing is our own direct-EXMC implementation, intentionally divergent — justified-NA.

verdicts.name: scope_draw_grid_dots
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_08018fcc (v2.c:5764) is a 4-bit-per-pixel glyph/bitmap BLIT: it reads nibbles from a source bitmap (param_5), converts via VectorUnsignedToFloat, scales to an alpha (fVar10/15*DAT), and alpha-blends each pixel into the RAM _framebuffer_ptr backbuffer with viewport clipping — an antialiased glyph blitter, not a 'grid dots' routine. Our firmware uses its OWN variable-width bitmap font system (scripts/generate_font.py + glyph render in main.c) drawing straight to EXMC, no 4-bit alpha framebuffer blit.
verdicts.justification: Decoded as an antialiased glyph/bitmap blitter into a software backbuffer; our font/UI rendering is original and direct-EXMC — justified-NA, not a gap.

verdicts.name: lcd_fill_rect_with_color
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_08019134 (v2.c:5844) computes a per-column HSV->RGB565 RAINBOW gradient (FUN_0803d540 = hue function, ramps fVar8 across the span and packs 5*/6*/11* into RGB565) and writes it in a strided 5-pixel dotted pattern into the RAM _framebuffer_ptr backbuffer with viewport clipping — a decorative gradient/dither fill, NOT a plain solid rect fill as the name implies. Our equivalent solid fill is firmware/src/drivers/lcd.c:466 lcd_fill_rect() which lcd_set_window()s and streams a constant color to EXMC 0x60020000 — our own implementation, no gradient, no backbuffer.
verdicts.justification: Decoded as a software-framebuffer rainbow/dither fill; our lcd_fill_rect is an original direct-EXMC solid fill, intentionally not byte-faithful — justified-NA.

verdicts.name: scope_draw_cursor
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_08019330 (v2.c:5936) reads existing pixels from the RAM _framebuffer_ptr backbuffer over a rect and applies a fixed-point RGB565 color TRANSFORM (the 0x80808081 reciprocal-multiply constants are /255 normalizations on the R/G/B channels) — an in-place tint/desaturate of a region, not a literal cursor sprite. It operates entirely on the software backbuffer with viewport clipping. Our firmware draws scope cursors directly to EXMC in main.c draw_scope_screen with our own logic and no read-modify-write backbuffer transform.
verdicts.justification: Decoded as a software-backbuffer region color-transform; our cursor/overlay drawing is original and direct-EXMC — justified-NA, not a gap.

verdicts.name: lcd_read_data_word
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_08019e00 (v2.c:6400) is one instruction: 'return uRam60020000;' — a read of the EXMC memory-mapped LCD DATA register at 0x60020000 (A17=high => RS/DCX=data). Our firmware firmware/src/drivers/lcd.c:153 lcd_read_data() does exactly 'return *LCD_DATA_ADDR;' where LCD_DATA_ADDR is the same 0x60020000 EXMC data address (header comment lcd.c:15 'Data register: 0x60020000'). Identical single EXMC-mapped read; same ST7789V-over-EXMC bus behavior.
verdicts.justification: Both are a bare read of the same EXMC-mapped 0x60020000 data register; register op is verified equivalent, so faithful — though hardware read-back is bench-untested it is a trivial 1-instruction mirror.

verdicts.name: freertos_task_idle_hook
verdicts.evidence: MISLABEL: FUN_08034828 (v2.c:8268) is NOT a FreeRTOS idle hook. It calls a registered draw callback (*_DAT_20036b80)(originX + bbox[0], originY + bbox[2], bbox[1]-bbox[0]+1, bbox[3]-bbox[2]+1, color) — a blit-dispatch over a 4-element bounding box. _DAT_20036b84/_b88 are the draw origin set in the JPEG/image draw path (v2.c:27058, next to _font_table_ptr and FUN_08030524 the JPEG Huffman decoder). It is UI/image rendering glue, not a kernel primitive. Our firmware sets configUSE_IDLE_HOOK=0 (firmware/include/FreeRTOSConfig.h:52) and has no such callback dispatcher; UI rendering is our own (main.c draw_* + lcd.c).
verdicts.justification: Decoded enough to prove the 'freertos_' name is wrong — it's an app-level draw-callback dispatcher, not a kernel hook; our UI is original and we don't use an idle hook, so justified-NA.

verdicts.name: freertos_list_insert_sorted
verdicts.evidence: FUN_080349b8 (v2.c:8345) IS the FreeRTOS kernel primitive prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList (firmware/FreeRTOS/tasks.c:8590). Match: top uxListRemove of xStateListItem (FUN_0803a024(_DAT_200061e0+4); clears ready-priority bit _DAT_200061ec on empty); param_1==-1 && param_2!=0 path = (xTicksToWait==portMAX_DELAY && xCanBlockIndefinitely) => listINSERT_END to suspended list (FUN_0803a118(&DAT_200062b0,...)); else xTimeToWake = xTickCount(_DAT_200062d4)+xTicksToWait, overflow check uVar3<uVar1 => vListInsert into overflow list _DAT_200062c8 (tasks.c:8644), else delayed list _DAT_200062c4 (tasks.c:8651) and update xNextTaskUnblockTime _DAT_200062d0 (tasks.c:8656). We compile this exact source (firmware/FreeRTOS/tasks.c, same vListInsert in list.c).
verdicts.justification: Behavior matches prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList line-for-line against our vendored tasks.c; it's literally the same upstream kernel code we build — NA_SAME_SOURCE, not the misleading 'list_insert_sorted' app name.

verdicts.name: freertos_timer_command
verdicts.evidence: Stock decompile (decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:28384) matches prvAddNewTaskToReadyList (single-core) in firmware/FreeRTOS/tasks.c:2130-2181: taskENTER_CRITICAL, ++uxCurrentNumberOfTasks (_DAT_20006254), if pxCurrentTCB==NULL(_DAT_200061e0) and count==1 call prvInitialiseTaskLists (FUN_080350d0), priority compare (+0x2c=uxPriority), set ready bitmap _DAT_200061ec = 1<<prio, vListInsertEnd into pxReadyTasksLists[prio] (0x200061f0 + prio*0x14 via FUN_0803a118), then PendSV (_DAT_e000ed04=0x10000000) if scheduler running.
verdicts.justification: Byte-for-byte the kernel's prvAddNewTaskToReadyList; the 'timer_command' name is a Ghidra mislabel, but it is the same upstream code we compile. new_D=3.

verdicts.name: freertos_timer_pend_call
verdicts.evidence: decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:28418 matches prvCheckForValidListAndQueue in firmware/FreeRTOS/timers.c:1114-1141: under critical section, if xTimerQueue(_DAT_200062e4)==NULL, vListInitialise x2 (FUN_0803a06c on 0x200062f8/0x2000630c = xActiveTimerList1/2), set pxCurrentTimerList(_DAT_200062f0)/pxOverflowTimerList(_DAT_200062ec), create xTimerQueue=xQueueGenericCreate(0xf,0xc,0) (15 items x 12 = configTIMER_QUEUE_LENGTH x sizeof(DaemonTaskMessage_t)).
verdicts.justification: Genuine timer-service init primitive; 'timer_pend_call' name is wrong but it is the same vendored timers.c we build. new_D=3.

verdicts.name: freertos_timer_process_expired
verdicts.evidence: decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:28441 matches prvCheckTasksWaitingTermination (single-core) in firmware/FreeRTOS/tasks.c:6110-6139: while(uxDeletedTasksWaitingCleanUp(_DAT_200062cc)!=0){ critical; pxTCB=head of xTasksWaitingTermination (_DAT_200062a8+0xc); uxListRemove(FUN_0803a024 on &xStateListItem at +4); --uxCurrentNumberOfTasks(_DAT_20006254); --cleanup; exit critical; prvDeleteTCB(FUN_08034d90) }.
verdicts.justification: Idle-task termination cleanup loop, exact kernel match; name is a mislabel (it processes terminated tasks, not expired timers) but same upstream tasks.c. new_D=3.

verdicts.name: freertos_task_set_timeout
verdicts.evidence: decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:28464 matches prvCopyDataFromQueue in firmware/FreeRTOS/queue.c:2476-2494: if uxItemSize(param_1[0x10])!=0, advance pcReadFrom(param_1[3]) by uxItemSize, wrap at pcTail(param_1[2]) back to pcHead(*param_1), memcpy(FUN_080017a6) into pvBuffer(param_2).
verdicts.justification: Exact prvCopyDataFromQueue; the 'task_set_timeout' name is a Ghidra mislabel, but it is the queue.c we compile. new_D=3.

verdicts.name: freertos_queue_copy_item
verdicts.evidence: decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:28483 matches prvCopyDataToQueue in firmware/FreeRTOS/queue.c:2393-2472: uxItemSize(param_1[0x10])==0 -> mutex disinherit on xMutexHolder(param_1[2]) via xTaskPriorityDisinherit(FUN_0803b934); xPosition==SEND_TO_BACK(param_3==0) -> memcpy to pcWriteTo(param_1[1]) + wrap at pcTail; else memcpy to pcReadFrom(param_1[3]) - uxItemSize + wrap, OVERWRITE(param_3==2) decrements; uxMessagesWaiting(param_1[0xe])++.
verdicts.justification: Exact prvCopyDataToQueue including the OVERWRITE/BACK/FRONT branches; name here is actually correct and it is the same vendored queue.c. new_D=3.

verdicts.name: freertos_timer_reload
verdicts.evidence: decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:28524 matches prvDeleteTCB in firmware/FreeRTOS/tasks.c: vPortFree(*(param_1+0x30)) frees the stack (pxStack at TCB+0x30), then vPortFree(param_1) frees the TCB. Called only from prvCheckTasksWaitingTermination (FUN_08034c24).
verdicts.justification: Exact prvDeleteTCB (two vPortFree calls, stack then TCB); 'timer_reload' name is a mislabel but it is the kernel tasks.c we build. new_D=3.

verdicts.name: freertos_task_increment_tick
verdicts.evidence: full_decompile.c:26753 matches prvGetHighestPriorityOfWaitToReceiveList in firmware/FreeRTOS/queue.c:2368-2388: if listCURRENT_LIST_LENGTH(&xTasksWaitingToReceive)(count at Queue_t+0x24) > 0 return configMAX_PRIORITIES(==5, FreeRTOSConfig.h:26) - listGET_ITEM_VALUE_OF_HEAD_ENTRY (= 5 - **(param_1+0x30)), else 0 (tskIDLE_PRIORITY). Caller (xQueueGenericSend, full_decompile.c:32390) feeds result to vTaskPriorityDisinheritAfterTimeout.
verdicts.justification: Not xTaskIncrementTick at all (that is the much larger FUN at tasks.c:4736); the literal 5 = configMAX_PRIORITIES nails it as prvGetHighestPriorityOfWaitToReceiveList. Same vendored queue.c. new_D=3.

verdicts.name: freertos_port_yield_from_isr
verdicts.evidence: full_decompile.c:26776 matches prvGetNextExpireTime in firmware/FreeRTOS/timers.c:840-864: *pxListWasEmpty(*param_1) = listLIST_IS_EMPTY(pxCurrentTimerList) (*_DAT_200062f0==0; _DAT_200062f0 set to pxCurrentTimerList in FUN_08034bc0); if not empty return listGET_ITEM_VALUE_OF_HEAD_ENTRY (_DAT_200062f0[3]) else 0. NOT a port yield (port.c xPortPendSVHandler/vPortYield live elsewhere).
verdicts.justification: The 'port_yield_from_isr'/hardware classification is provably wrong: this is pure timer-service code (prvGetNextExpireTime) from the vendored timers.c, no register/ISR ops. Reclassified NA_SAME_SOURCE, new_D=3.

verdicts.name: freertos_timer_create_static
verdicts.evidence: Stock body sets two free-list head pointers to &DAT_20008330, block size 0x1ff0, and xFreeBytesRemaining/xMinimumEverFreeBytesRemaining (DAT_20006324=1) — byte-for-byte the prvHeapInit logic in /Users/david/Projects/RESEARCH/osc/firmware/FreeRTOS/portable/MemMang/heap_4.c:456-501 (xStart.pxNextFreeBlock, pxEnd, pxFirstFreeBlock->xBlockSize). Makefile:136 compiles heap_4.c into our build.
verdicts.justification: This is prvHeapInit from heap_4.c, the identical upstream source we compile — the assigned name 'timer_create_static' is a Ghidra mislabel; not a timer fn and not a gap.

verdicts.name: freertos_timer_start_from_isr
verdicts.evidence: Stock: if(uxItemSize==0) pcHead=pxQueue else pcHead=pucQueueStorage; writes uxLength([0xf]) and uxItemSize([0x10]); then xQueueReset(q,pdTRUE). Exact match to prvInitialiseNewQueue in /Users/david/Projects/RESEARCH/osc/firmware/FreeRTOS/queue.c:567-596 (pcHead set, uxLength/uxItemSize, xQueueGenericReset). queue.c is vendored and compiled.
verdicts.justification: This is prvInitialiseNewQueue (queue.c), same upstream source; 'timer_start_from_isr' is a mislabel. Verified by behavior not name.

verdicts.name: freertos_timer_daemon_init
verdicts.evidence: Stock copies up to 16 chars of a name into TCB+0x34 (pcTaskName), clamps priority to 4 (configMAX_PRIORITIES-1) writing uxPriority/uxBasePriority, calls FUN_0803a09c (vListInitialiseItem) twice for xStateListItem/xEventListItem, builds the stack via FUN_08035eac (pxPortInitialiseStack), and stores top-of-stack at TCB[0]. This is prvInitialiseNewTask in /Users/david/Projects/RESEARCH/osc/firmware/FreeRTOS/tasks.c:1816+ (vendored, compiled).
verdicts.justification: prvInitialiseNewTask (tasks.c), same kernel source — name-copy + priority clamp + listItem init + stack init all match. 'timer_daemon_init' is a mislabel.

verdicts.name: freertos_timer_pend_callback
verdicts.evidence: Two-path kernel routine: setup branch initialises a control block (writes [0],[6],[7],[8], FUN_0803a09c=vListInitialiseItem, sets bit 0x4 flag) and the alternate path is prvTaskExitError — raises BASEPRI to configMAX_SYSCALL_INT (0x10) via setBasePriority under isCurrentModePrivileged then DSB/ISB + infinite loop. This is the Cortex-M port-layer task/stack init helper (port.c / tasks.c) which we vendor and compile (firmware/FreeRTOS/portable + tasks.c).
verdicts.justification: Kernel/port primitive (prvTaskExitError + new-task setup) from the same FreeRTOS source we compile; 'timer_pend_callback' is a mislabel. NA, not a gap.

verdicts.name: freertos_timer_execute_callback
verdicts.evidence: Stock loops local_14=0..4 calling FUN_0803a06c (vListInitialise) on 5 lists at 0x200061f0 stride 0x14 (pxReadyTasksLists[0..4], configMAX_PRIORITIES=5), then 5 more vListInitialise on the delayed/overflow/pending-ready/suspended lists, and sets _DAT_200062c4/200062c8 = pxDelayedTaskList/pxOverflowDelayedTaskList. Exact prvInitialiseTaskLists shape in /Users/david/Projects/RESEARCH/osc/firmware/FreeRTOS/tasks.c:6078.
verdicts.justification: prvInitialiseTaskLists (tasks.c), identical vendored source; the 5+5 vListInitialise pattern is unmistakable. 'timer_execute_callback' is a mislabel.

verdicts.name: freertos_timer_switch_lists
verdicts.evidence: Stock walks the free-block list from &DAT_2000632c (xStart) to find insertion point, then coalesces with the preceding block (puc+xBlockSize==insert) and following block, with a pxEnd special-case (_DAT_20006320). Byte-faithful to prvInsertBlockIntoFreeList in /Users/david/Projects/RESEARCH/osc/firmware/FreeRTOS/portable/MemMang/heap_4.c:504-565 (iterate, merge-before, merge-after-unless-pxEnd, conditional relink).
verdicts.justification: prvInsertBlockIntoFreeList (heap_4.c), same upstream source we compile; the two-sided coalesce + pxEnd guard match exactly. 'timer_switch_lists' is a mislabel.

verdicts.name: freertos_queue_item_init
verdicts.evidence: Stock sets list-item value *(p+4)=value and owner *(p+0x10)=p, then performs overflow-aware wake-time insertion: when value wraps it inserts into the overflow list (FUN_0803a0ac on _DAT_200062f0) else the delayed list (_DAT_200062ec), returning whether the head was updated. This is the list-insert/delayed-list insertion primitive of vListInsert / prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList (firmware/FreeRTOS/list.c:vListInsert + tasks.c:8590). Called from the scheduler insertion loops (decompile lines 28883/28921/28979).
verdicts.justification: Kernel list-insertion primitive (list.c/tasks.c), same vendored source; sets xItemValue+pvOwner then ordered/overflow insert. 'queue_item_init' is a partial mislabel but still NA_SAME_SOURCE.

verdicts.name: freertos_list_remove_head
verdicts.evidence: Stock: taskENTER_CRITICAL; read *(queue+0x38) (uxMessagesWaiting); taskEXIT_CRITICAL; return ==0. Called from xQueueGenericReceive context (decompile 34204-34221 shows prvLockQueue cRxLock/cTxLock at +0x44/+0x45, vTaskSuspendAll). Matches prvIsQueueEmpty in /Users/david/Projects/RESEARCH/osc/firmware/FreeRTOS/queue.c:2617 (uxMessagesWaiting==0 under critical section).
verdicts.justification: prvIsQueueEmpty (queue.c), same upstream source we compile; it is a queue-empty check, NOT list_remove_head — the assigned name is wrong, but classification is NA_SAME_SOURCE.

verdicts.name: freertos_queue_check_space
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_080352d4 (decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:28830): taskENTER_CRITICAL(); reads two struct fields at +0x38/+0x3c, returns equality, taskEXIT_CRITICAL(). This is a timers.c list/active helper (Timer_t status compare under critical), part of the software-timer service whose daemon is freertos_timer_service_task@0803579c. Vendored source firmware/FreeRTOS/timers.c is compiled (Makefile:134, configUSE_TIMERS=1).
verdicts.justification: NAME IS A MISLABEL — it is not a queue-space check; it is a timers.c critical-section field compare. Same upstream FreeRTOS source we compile, so NA_SAME_SOURCE is honest; decoded to confirm it is not app code.

verdicts.name: freertos_port_disable_interrupts
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_08035304 (v2.c:28851) is an MPU-wrapped prvProcessExpiredTimer/prvInsertTimerInActiveList helper: privilege/MSP/MPU setup (isCurrentModePrivileged/setBasePriority/DSB/ISB) then FUN_08035224=prvInsertTimerInActiveList re-insert and FUN_0803be38 list op — matches timers.c:1046 'prvInsertTimerInActiveList(pxTimer, xTimeNow+period, ...)'. firmware/FreeRTOS/timers.c + the GCC ARM_CM4F port (portable/GCC/ARM_CM4F/port.c) are vendored and compiled.
verdicts.justification: NAME IS A MISLABEL — not a port interrupt-disable; it is the MPU/privilege-wrapped expired-timer reinsert in timers.c. Identical upstream kernel source, so NA_SAME_SOURCE; decoded to D3 to rule out app code.

verdicts.name: freertos_task_create_static
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_0803532c (v2.c:28911) mirrors FUN_08035304 with an offset param: FUN_0803a024 list helper, FUN_08035224=prvInsertTimerInActiveList(iVar+0x18+param), FUN_0803be38 list op, privilege/setBasePriority(0x10) tail. This is prvProcessExpiredTimer (timers.c:714) re-inserting an auto-reload timer (xExpiredTime+period pattern, timers.c:702). Vendored timers.c compiled (Makefile:134).
verdicts.justification: NAME IS A MISLABEL — not xTaskCreateStatic; it is timers.c prvProcessExpiredTimer/insert. Same kernel source we compile -> NA_SAME_SOURCE; decoded to D3.

verdicts.name: freertos_scheduler_start
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_080353b4 (v2.c:28949) = prvProcessReceivedCommands: xQueueReceive loop on the timer command queue with switch cases 0..9 matching timers.h tmrCOMMAND_START_DONT_TRACE(0)..CHANGE_PERIOD_FROM_ISR(9); case 5 does vPortFree / ucStatus&~ACTIVE = tmrCOMMAND_DELETE (timers.c:1049). Called directly by freertos_timer_service_task@0803579c (v2.c:29214). Vendored firmware/FreeRTOS/timers.c compiled, configUSE_TIMERS=1.
verdicts.justification: NAME IS A MISLABEL — not vTaskStartScheduler; it is timers.c prvProcessReceivedCommands (command codes 0-9 verified against timers.h). Same upstream source -> NA_SAME_SOURCE; D3.

verdicts.name: freertos_task_create
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_08035504 (v2.c:29035) = prvProcessTimerOrBlockTask: vTaskSuspendAll(); FUN_080355dc=prvSampleTimeNow(&listWasSwitched); if not switched, either process expired (FUN_0803532c) or block the daemon (FUN_0803a32c=vTaskPlaceOnEventListRestricted) and on xTaskResumeAll==0 write ICSR _DAT_e000ed04=0x10000000 (PendSV set) + DSB/ISB. Called by freertos_timer_service_task@0803579c (v2.c:29213). timers.c compiled (Makefile:134).
verdicts.justification: NAME IS A MISLABEL — not xTaskCreate; it is timers.c prvProcessTimerOrBlockTask (ICSR PendSV-set + suspend-all confirm it is the daemon block path). Same kernel source -> NA_SAME_SOURCE; D3.

verdicts.name: freertos_critical_nesting_inc
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_080355a0 (v2.c:29080) = prvGetNextExpireTime: if pxCurrentTimerList empty (*_DAT_200062c4==0) set result _DAT_200062d0=0xffffffff (portMAX_DELAY/listWasEmpty path), else result = value of head list item (*_DAT_200062c4[3]). Exactly timers.c:840 prvGetNextExpireTime. timers.c vendored+compiled.
verdicts.justification: NAME IS A MISLABEL — not a critical-nesting increment; it is timers.c prvGetNextExpireTime (0xffffffff empty-list sentinel is the tell). Same upstream source -> NA_SAME_SOURCE; D3.

verdicts.name: freertos_scheduler_suspend
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_080355dc (v2.c:29100) = prvSampleTimeNow: uVar1=FUN_0803b768=xTaskGetTickCount(); if(uVar1 < xLastTime _DAT_200062f4){ FUN_08035620=prvSwitchTimerLists(); *pxSwitched=1 } else *pxSwitched=0; _DAT_200062f4=uVar1. Matches timers.c:867 prvSampleTimeNow line-for-line (xTimeNow<xLastTime -> prvSwitchTimerLists). timers.c compiled.
verdicts.justification: NAME IS A MISLABEL — not vTaskSuspendAll; it is timers.c prvSampleTimeNow (overflow detect -> switch lists). Same kernel source -> NA_SAME_SOURCE; D3.

verdicts.name: freertos_scheduler_resume
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_08035620 (v2.c:29125) = prvSwitchTimerLists: while current timer list non-empty, process head (prvProcessExpiredTimer via the +0x20 callback + reinsert FUN_0803a0ac), then swap the two list pointers _DAT_200062f0<->_DAT_200062ec. This is timers.c:1089 prvSwitchTimerLists (pxTemp=pxCurrentTimerList; pxCurrentTimerList=pxOverflowTimerList; pxOverflowTimerList=pxTemp). Called by prvSampleTimeNow@080355dc. timers.c compiled (Makefile:134).
verdicts.justification: NAME IS A MISLABEL — not xTaskResumeAll; it is timers.c prvSwitchTimerLists (the current/overflow pointer swap is unmistakable). Same upstream source -> NA_SAME_SOURCE; D3.

verdicts.name: freertos_task_get_current
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_08035730 = FreeRTOS prvTaskIsTaskSuspended (tasks.c:3323). param_1==0 + ISB/DSB + infinite spin = configASSERT(xTask) failure handler. *(p+0x14)==&DAT_200062b0 = listIS_CONTAINED_WITHIN(&xSuspendedTaskList, xStateListItem); returns whether xEventListItem.pxContainer(0x28)==0 and !=xPendingReadyList(&DAT_20006288) — byte-exact to tasks.c:3335-3368. Same kernel compiled at /Users/david/Projects/RESEARCH/osc/firmware/FreeRTOS/tasks.c.
verdicts.justification: The given name 'freertos_task_get_current' is a mislabel; decoded the actual body to prvTaskIsTaskSuspended and confirmed offset-for-offset against our vendored tasks.c — literally the same upstream code.

verdicts.name: freertos_ready_list_add
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_080357b8 = FreeRTOS prvUnlockQueue (queue.c:183, static; called at queue.c:1133+). taskENTER_CRITICAL, walks cTxLock count at byte 0x45 over xTasksWaitingToReceive list (p+0x24) calling xTaskRemoveFromEventList (FUN_0803bb4c) + vTaskMissedYield (FUN_0803a424=_DAT_200061e8=1), resets lock byte to 0xFF (queueUNLOCKED), then same for cRxLock(0x44)/xTasksWaitingToSend(0x10), taskEXIT_CRITICAL. Matches prvUnlockQueue structure exactly. /Users/david/Projects/RESEARCH/osc/firmware/FreeRTOS/queue.c.
verdicts.justification: Name 'freertos_ready_list_add' is a mislabel; the two-list-walk + queueUNLOCKED(0xFF) reset + xTaskRemoveFromEventList + vTaskMissedYield is unambiguously prvUnlockQueue — same kernel we compile.

verdicts.name: fpga_usart_tx_task
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_08037400 (full_decompile.c:28542): infinite loop, xQueueReceive (FUN_0803b1d8, portMAX_DELAY) of a cmd word, writes word bytes into a frame, computes checksum (byte2+byte3 stored at frame+9), kicks USART2 (uRam40000000|=0x80), vTaskDelay(10). Our own impl: /Users/david/Projects/RESEARCH/osc/firmware/src/drivers/fpga.c:1171 fpga_usart_tx_task — same role (blocking xQueueReceive on usart_tx_queue, builds 10B frame, frame[2]=cmd_hi/frame[3]=cmd_lo/frame[9]=(hi+lo)&0xFF checksum). Stock differs: it pumps via a polled buffer; ours is interrupt-driven (USART2->ctrl1 |= TDBEIEN, ISR drains tx_frame).
verdicts.justification: This is app protocol code, not kernel/libc — our firmware has its own original dvom_TX task that faithfully reproduces the 10-byte frame+checksum protocol but is not byte-faithful (IRQ-driven vs polled). Justified-not-needed, not a gap.

verdicts.name: fpga_spi3_transfer
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_08037800 (full_decompile.c:28575, 472B): Ghidra decode is register-context-corrupt — enters via a `code_r0x08037800:` label with live-but-undefined inbound registers (unaff_r7=arg ptr, unaff_r9=struct base, in_ZR, and unaff_s16..s28 VFP scratch), so the body (writes _DAT_40010c10/14=0x40 = SPI3, FP scaling) cannot be trusted statement-for-statement. Our counterpart is the SPI3 path in /Users/david/Projects/RESEARCH/osc/firmware/src/drivers/fpga.c, but equivalence is bench-gated (FPGA config-entry wall, see MEMORY fpga-config-entry-wall.md).
verdicts.justification: Per the prompt's explicit flag and verified here: corrupt disasm context blocks faithful decode, and equivalence depends on un-completed bench bring-up. Next action: re-decompile with corrected ABI/register seed, then bench-diff SPI3 ops vs a stock Saleae capture. Honestly cannot stamp resolved.

verdicts.name: freertos_list_remove
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_0803a024 = FreeRTOS uxListRemove (list.c:217). pxContainer at p+0x10; relinks pxNext->pxPrevious (p+4->+8) and pxPrevious->pxNext (p+8->+4); if pxList->pxIndex(piVar1[1])==item set pxIndex=pxPrevious; clears pxContainer(+0x10)=0; decrements uxNumberOfItems(*piVar1) and returns it. Byte-exact to /Users/david/Projects/RESEARCH/osc/firmware/FreeRTOS/list.c:225-246.
verdicts.justification: Name is correct and the offsets map 1:1 to uxListRemove in our vendored list.c — same upstream code.

verdicts.name: freertos_port_enter_critical
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_0803a06c = FreeRTOS vListInitialise (list.c:49), NOT a critical-section primitive. param_1[1]=param_1+2 (pxIndex=&xListEnd); param_1[2]=0xffffffff (xListEnd.xItemValue=portMAX_DELAY); param_1[3]=param_1[4]=param_1+2 (xListEnd pxNext/pxPrevious=self); *param_1=0 (uxNumberOfItems=0). Matches list.c:56-78.
verdicts.justification: FLAG: the label 'freertos_port_enter_critical' is a mislabel — decoded body is vListInitialise. Still NA_SAME_SOURCE (kernel list primitive in our FreeRTOS/list.c), so resolved, but the ledger name should be corrected.

verdicts.name: freertos_port_exit_critical
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_0803a09c = FreeRTOS vListInitialiseItem (list.c:89), NOT exit-critical. Single statement: *(param_1+0x10)=0 → pxItem->pxContainer=NULL. Matches list.c:94.
verdicts.justification: FLAG: 'freertos_port_exit_critical' is a mislabel — body is vListInitialiseItem (clears pxContainer). NA_SAME_SOURCE kernel primitive in our FreeRTOS/list.c; resolved, but name should be corrected.

verdicts.name: freertos_list_insert_end
verdicts.evidence: Stock FUN_0803a0ac = FreeRTOS vListInsert (SORTED insert, list.c:139), NOT vListInsertEnd. If *param_2(xItemValue)==0xffffffff use xListEnd.pxPrevious; else walk `for(it=&xListEnd; it->pxNext->xItemValue <= xValue; it=it->pxNext)`; then splice param_2 in, set pxContainer(p2+0x10... here param_2[4])=pxList, uxNumberOfItems++. The <= sorted loop is the signature of vListInsert (list.c:161-210), distinct from vListInsertEnd.
verdicts.justification: FLAG: 'freertos_list_insert_end' is a mislabel — the value-sorted <= iteration is vListInsert, not vListInsertEnd (which does no sorting). NA_SAME_SOURCE kernel primitive in our FreeRTOS/list.c; resolved, but name should be corrected.

verdicts.name: freertos_list_init
verdicts.evidence: Stock body (decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:32949-32962) inserts param_2 before pxIndex's predecessor and does `*param_1 = *param_1 + 1`. This is byte-for-byte vListInsertEnd, matching FreeRTOS/list.c:105-130 (pxNewListItem->pxNext = pxIndex; pxIndex->pxPrevious->pxNext = pxNewListItem; uxNumberOfItems++). Confirmed callers are vTaskSuspend/scheduler list ops.
verdicts.justification: Genuine FreeRTOS list primitive we compile from the vendored list.c; batch name 'list_init' is a mislabel (it is vListInsertEnd), but classification is unchanged - same upstream source.

verdicts.name: freertos_port_save_context
verdicts.evidence: Stock (decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:32970-32975) is a one-liner `*DAT_0803a164 |= 0xf00000`, called ONLY from xPortStartScheduler (FUN_0803a9d4 @0803a9d4) just after SysTick/SHPR setup and before CPACR VFP enable (e000ef34 |= 0xc0000000). It is a Cortex-M4F port-layer register-setup helper in the scheduler-start path, compiled from FreeRTOS/portable/GCC/ARM_CM4F/port.c (xPortStartScheduler @port.c:305).
verdicts.justification: Not 'save_context' (that is the naked xPortPendSVHandler asm); this is a small port.c register-init helper on the start-scheduler path - still identical upstream port source we compile, so NA_SAME_SOURCE.

verdicts.name: freertos_timer_start
verdicts.evidence: Ghidra labels this vTaskStartScheduler; stock (decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:33430-33464) does xTaskCreate(idle task), creates the timer task (FUN_0803bdd4), sets uxSchedulerSuspended/xSchedulerRunning globals, then calls xPortStartScheduler (FUN_0803a9d4). Exact shape of FreeRTOS/tasks.c vTaskStartScheduler. Not a timer-start routine.
verdicts.justification: Genuine tasks.c vTaskStartScheduler compiled from our vendored tasks.c; batch name 'timer_start' is a mislabel but same upstream source.

verdicts.name: freertos_systick_handler
verdicts.evidence: Ghidra labels this vTaskSuspend; stock (decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:33474-33531) takes critical section, removes TCB from ready/event lists, clears the ready-priority bit (uxTopReadyPriority &= ~(1<<uxPriority)), inserts onto xSuspendedTaskList (vListInsertEnd @0803a118), and yields if suspending the running task. This is FreeRTOS/tasks.c vTaskSuspend, NOT a SysTick ISR (the real SysTick handler is the naked xPortSysTickHandler in port.c).
verdicts.justification: Genuine tasks.c vTaskSuspend; batch name 'systick_handler' is a clear mislabel but same upstream kernel source we compile.

verdicts.name: freertos_port_yield
verdicts.evidence: Stock (decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:33541-33545) is just `_DAT_200061e4 = _DAT_200061e4 + 1;` - i.e. ++uxSchedulerSuspended. Exactly FreeRTOS/tasks.c vTaskSuspendAll. Port yield is instead a write to ICSR (e000ed04 = 0x10000000), which appears elsewhere (e.g. in vTaskSuspend at line 33525).
verdicts.justification: Genuine tasks.c vTaskSuspendAll (single global increment); batch name 'port_yield' is a mislabel but same upstream source.

verdicts.name: freertos_port_pendsv_handler
verdicts.evidence: Stock (decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:33556-33586) uses `0x1f - LZCOUNT(uxTopReadyPriority)` to index the ready-task array and advance pxIndex to the next ready TCB, setting pxCurrentTCB (_DAT_200061e0). This is vTaskSwitchContext via portGET_HIGHEST_PRIORITY/taskSELECT_HIGHEST_PRIORITY_TASK (FreeRTOS/tasks.c:5120-5202, CLZ macro at tasks.c:236-241). The PendSV handler proper is the naked xPortPendSVHandler in port.c, which CALLS this.
verdicts.justification: Genuine tasks.c vTaskSwitchContext (CLZ priority select); batch name 'pendsv_handler' is a mislabel but same upstream kernel source we compile.

verdicts.name: freertos_timer_delete
verdicts.evidence: Ghidra labels this xPortStartScheduler. Stock (decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:33597-33681) checks CPUID e000ed00 against 0x410fc270/0x410fc271 (CM4 r0p0/r0p1 errata asserts), sets SHPR3 e000ed20 |= 0xf0f00000 (PendSV+SysTick lowest priority), SysTick LOAD e000e014 = 239999 (240MHz/1kHz-1), CTRL e000e010 = 7, clears critical nesting, enables VFP via CPACR e000ef34 |= 0xc0000000. Matches FreeRTOS/portable/GCC/ARM_CM4F/port.c xPortStartScheduler (port.c:305, SHPR3 lines 433-434, SysTick setup line 828, vPortEnableVFP). Not a timer-delete routine.
verdicts.justification: Genuine ARM_CM4F port.c xPortStartScheduler; batch name 'timer_delete' is a mislabel but same upstream port source we compile.

verdicts.name: freertos_timer_reset
verdicts.evidence: Ghidra labels this xQueueGenericCreate (decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:33689+). Signature xQueueGenericCreate(uxQueueLength, uxItemSize, ucQueueType) with the length!=0 assert and pvPortMalloc of (length*itemSize + queue struct). Exactly FreeRTOS/queue.c xQueueGenericCreate. Not a timer-reset routine.
verdicts.justification: Genuine queue.c xQueueGenericCreate; batch name 'timer_reset' is a mislabel but same upstream kernel source we compile.

verdicts.name: freertos_timer_stop
verdicts.evidence: full_decompile.c:31767 FUN_0803ac38 is NOT timer_stop; it is FreeRTOS xQueueGenericReset (queue.c): recomputes pcTail=pcHead+uxLength*uxItemSize (p1[2]), uxMessagesWaiting=0 (p1[0xe]), pcWriteTo=pcHead (p1[1]), pcReadFrom (p1[3]), cRxLock/cTxLock=queueUNLOCKED(0xff), and on xNewQueue!=0 re-inits the two waiting List_t at +4/+9 else yields if tasks waiting. queue.c present and compiled at firmware/FreeRTOS/queue.c (grep xQueueGenericReset present).
verdicts.justification: Literal upstream kernel primitive we compile; the 'timer_stop' name is a Ghidra mislabel. Decoded to register/offset level confirming xQueueGenericReset, not assumed from name.

verdicts.name: xQueueGenericSend
verdicts.evidence: decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:33799 matches FreeRTOS queue.c xQueueGenericSend exactly: copy-to-queue FUN_08034cc4(prvCopyDataToQueue), uxMessagesWaiting<uxLength gate or xCopyPosition==2(overwrite), notify waiting receivers list at +0x24 then PendSV (_DAT_e000ed04=0x10000000), block-with-timeout via vTaskSuspendAll/prvUnlockQueue(+0x44/+0x45 locks)/xTaskCheckForTimeOut. firmware/FreeRTOS/queue.c compiled.
verdicts.justification: Exact upstream queue.c primitive we vendor and compile; structure (locks, suspend, yield) is byte-faithful to FreeRTOS, name already correct.

verdicts.name: xQueueGenericSendFromISR
verdicts.evidence: decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:33933 is FreeRTOS queue.c xQueueGenericSendFromISR: masks ISR via setBasePriority(0x10)=configMAX_SYSCALL_INTERRUPT_PRIORITY (portSET_INTERRUPT_MASK_FROM_ISR), gate uxMessagesWaiting<uxLength, prvCopyDataToQueue, increments cTxLock at +0x45 when queue locked (cVar1!=-1), else notifies receiver list at +0x24 and sets *pxHigherPriorityTaskWoken. firmware/FreeRTOS/queue.c compiled.
verdicts.justification: Exact upstream ISR-variant queue primitive; cTxLock (+0x45) lock-defer logic is the FromISR signature, verified not assumed.

verdicts.name: freertos_queue_notify_from_isr
verdicts.evidence: decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:34022 FUN_0803b06c is NOT a notify; it is FreeRTOS uxQueueMessagesWaiting (queue.c): taskENTER_CRITICAL, returns p1[2]=uxMessagesWaiting when *p1==0 else 0, taskEXIT_CRITICAL. Caller at v2.c:30334 uses it on _osc_semaphore, checks ==0 to decide whether to xQueueGenericSend(give) — i.e. uxSemaphoreGetCount. firmware/FreeRTOS/queue.c compiled.
verdicts.justification: Upstream count-query primitive, mislabeled 'notify_from_isr'; verified via body (critical-section count read) and caller semantics (semaphore count check), not the name.

verdicts.name: xQueueReceiveFromISR
verdicts.evidence: decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:34044 is FreeRTOS queue.c xQueueReceiveFromISR: ISR mask via setBasePriority(0x10), checks uxMessagesWaiting (p1[0xe]<p1[0xf]), prvCopyDataFromQueue, increments cRxLock at +0x45 when locked, else unblocks sender list at +9 and sets pxHigherPriorityTaskWoken. firmware/FreeRTOS/queue.c compiled.
verdicts.justification: Exact upstream ISR receive primitive; cRxLock (+0x45) defer path is the FromISR fingerprint, decoded to offset level.

verdicts.name: xQueueReceive
verdicts.evidence: decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:34140 is FreeRTOS queue.c xQueueReceive: uxMessagesWaiting!=0 path does prvCopyDataFromQueue(FUN_08034c80), decrement (p1[0x38]-1), unblock senders list +0x10 and PendSV; else block-with-timeout via vTaskSuspendAll, prvUnlockQueue(+0x44/+0x45), xTaskCheckForTimeOut, vTaskPlaceOnEventList. firmware/FreeRTOS/queue.c compiled.
verdicts.justification: Exact upstream blocking-receive primitive we compile; name already correct, verified by the copy/decrement/block structure.

verdicts.name: freertos_tick_handler
verdicts.evidence: decompiled_2C53T_v2.c:34869 FUN_0803be38 is NOT a tick handler; it is FreeRTOS timers.c xTimerGenericCommand: guards on timer-service queue _DAT_200062e4 (xTimerQueue), builds a DaemonTaskMessage struct (local_30=cmd, local_2c=value, local_28=timer) and sends to the queue via xQueueGenericSend when xCommandID<6 (tmrCOMMAND_START..STOP, scheduler-running check FUN_0803b730==2), else xQueueGenericSendFromISR for the >=6 FromISR commands. firmware/FreeRTOS/timers.c compiled (xTimerGenericCommand present).
verdicts.justification: Upstream timer command dispatcher, mislabeled 'tick_handler'; verified via the <6 command-split and send-to-timer-queue structure, the literal xTimerGenericCommand fingerprint, not the name.

na_our_own: 08019134|HSV rainbow gradient fill, NOT plain rect fill (mislabel)
verify_faithful: 08019e00|lcd_read_data: bare read of EXMC 0x60020000 == our lcd.c:153 *LCD_DATA_ADDR
still_unresolved: 08037800|fpga_spi3_transfer: corrupt Ghidra register context + bench-gated equivalence (FPGA config-entry wall)
flags: 08019e00 (lcd_read_data) is the ONLY VERIFY_FAITHFUL and the only numerator growth in the batch, yet its own evidence admits 'hardware read-back is bench-untested'. It is a trivial 1-instruction EXMC mirror so equivalence is defensible at the register level, but stamping V1 on a bench-untested read is the single weakest 'resolved' verdict — verify against a stock capture or a live read before counting it as proven device behavior.

0803a154 (port_save_context -> 'xPortStartScheduler register helper'): vaguest evidence in the NA_SAME_SOURCE set. It is decoded only as a one-liner '*DAT |= 0xf00000' on the scheduler-start path; not pinned to a named upstream function. Still clearly port.c source we compile, so NA stands, but the exact line should be confirmed.

08035058 (timer_pend_callback -> 'prvTaskExitError + new-task setup'): describes TWO unrelated code paths in one verdict and does not name a single upstream function cleanly. Plausibly two adjacent functions Ghidra fused; NA_SAME_SOURCE is safe but the decode is the muddiest in the kernel batch.

08037800 (fpga_spi3_transfer): correctly left UNRESOLVED but it is assigned new_R=R2/new_V=V1 while resolved=false and classification=UNRESOLVED — contradictory ledger fields. The R/V scores should not advance on an unresolved, bench-gated, corrupt-disasm function. Treat R/V as null until re-decompiled and bench-diffed.

Systematic naming hazard: 40+ of 46 NA_SAME_SOURCE entries carry Ghidra names that are flat-out wrong (timer_* names mapping to queue/task/heap/list primitives, port_* names mapping to list-init or task-suspend). Classification (same vendored source) is sound and well-evidenced, but the ledger NAMES must be overwritten with the verified upstream symbol or future readers will mis-attribute these. The cluster 08034ab0-080357b8 and 0803a6d8-0803be38 are especially mislabeled.
tally.notes: Honest accounting of the gain: this batch is overwhelmingly DENOMINATOR-SHRINK, not numerator-growth. 61 of 63 verdicts are NA (46 NA_SAME_SOURCE + 15 NA_OUR_OWN) — they remove code from 'device behavior we must reimplement and prove': 46 are literally the FreeRTOS kernel/heap/port/queue/timer/list source we vendor and compile (verified: tasks.c, timers.c, queue.c, list.c, heap_4.c, GCC/ARM_CM4F/port.c all present and in Makefile; configMAX_PRIORITIES=5 and configUSE_TIMERS=1 corroborate the '5' and timer-service tells), and 15 are our own original UI/font/scope-draw/dvom_TX implementations (cross-checked against firmware/src: font.c, lcd.c, fpga.c). NUMERATOR GROWTH is exactly ONE function: 08019e00 lcd_read_data (VERIFY_FAITHFUL), and even that is a 1-instruction EXMC-register mirror that is bench-untested — so genuine proven-equivalent device behavior added this round is ~1 trivial function. 62 newly-resolved, 1 still unresolved (08037800 SPI3, correctly held open behind the FPGA config-entry wall). Caveat the user should carry forward: the resolved count is real but the 'meaningful denominator' is what shrank; do not read 62 resolutions as 62 drivers proven faithful. Secondary caveat: nearly every NA_SAME_SOURCE Ghidra name is wrong — classification is right, names need correcting in the ledger.
